source: Petitions. Nineteen senior members of the Malayan Customs Service__CO273:660:4
filename: Petitions. Nineteen senior members of the Malayan Customs Service__CO273:660:4.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** A petition (memorial) from **nineteen senior members of the Malayan Customs Service** seeking a **more favourable application of revised salary scales** in the Customs Department.  
- **Geography/time period:** Concerns **British Malaya** (notably the **Straits Settlements (S.S.)** and **Federated Malay States (F.M.S.)**); main correspondence and decisions span **1937–1940** (petition forwarded **29 July 1939**; replies and internal minutes in **Feb–June 1940**).  
- **Key decision/outcome:** After consultation, the **Governments of the Straits Settlements and Federated Malay States opposed granting concessions**, and the Colonial Office side agreed; the Secretary of State indicated he was **not prepared to intervene** to alter the revised scale for the petitioners.  
- **Rationale given:** The **1 Jan 1937** improvement to **initial salaries** for Customs (and Police) probationers was intended to raise **minimum starting pay** per the **MacGregor Committee/Commission recommendations**, with only necessary adjustments so that **no officer would be paid less than a junior**—**not** to provide a **general retrospective enhancement** for senior time-scale officers.  
- **Recruitment “cut-off” issue:** The file discusses a distinction between officers recruited **before vs. after mid-1931** (linked to changes in recruitment age/educational background); the committee view cited was that increases should **not** apply to **Customs probationers recruited prior to the latter half of 1931**.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Sir Shenton Thomas** (Governor/High Commissioner; forwarded memorial and consulted local governments), **S. W. Jones** (author of detailed explanatory letter), **G. E. J. Gent** (Colonial Office recipient), and the **Colonial Office/Secretary of State** (draft reply signed “**Lloyd**”); references to the **MacGregor Committee (Temporary Allowances, 1937)** and an implementing committee including senior Police and Customs officials.  
- **Notable numbers/dates:** Salary-scale changes effective **1 Jan 1937**; petition involves **19** senior Customs officers; discussion includes extension of the Customs time scale maximum from **$750 to $800 per month** (noted as a future benefit to officers).
